statusLine: European fuel security is under strain with 18 of 27 EU countries critically short on at least one major fuel type, despite average reserves meeting requirements.
fullAnalysis:
While EU-wide averages remain above the mandatory 90-day minimum for petrol (94.5 days) and diesel (95.8 days), the distribution is highly uneven across member states. Jet fuel reserves average just 89.9 days, falling short of requirements. Most concerning is that 18 of 27 countries have critical shortages in at least one fuel category, with Romania, Poland, and Spain facing critical levels across all three fuel types.

Diesel faces the most widespread pressure, with 15 countries holding critical reserves below 90 days. This is particularly problematic given diesel's central role in European logistics and heating. Countries like Romania (20.2 days) and Poland (71 days) face especially acute diesel shortages that could impact essential services. Jet fuel shortages in 16 countries, including major aviation hubs, pose risks to both commercial and cargo flights.

Price pressures are mounting with Brent crude at $96.74 per barrel, up 2.1% and roughly 27% higher since the Middle East conflict began. Average EU petrol prices have reached €1.789 per liter, with significant variation from Malta's €1.34 to the Netherlands' €2.334. These elevated prices, combined with strategic reserve releases due to supply disruptions, indicate ongoing supply chain stress that could worsen if geopolitical tensions escalate further.
keyPoints: 18 of 27 EU countries face critical shortages in at least one major fuel type, despite meeting overall EU averages
Diesel faces the broadest pressure with 15 countries below 90-day requirements, including Romania at just 20.2 days
Jet fuel reserves average 89.9 days EU-wide, falling short of the 90-day mandate with 16 countries in critical status
Oil prices have surged 27% since the Middle East conflict began, with Brent crude now at $96.74 per barrel
Eastern European countries including Romania, Poland, and Lithuania show the most acute shortages across multiple fuel categories
